slides.title: Vulca: make taste repeatable before the first public demo
slides.job: Create a public-demo opening that feels designed before explaining the system.

slides.title: The problem is not making slides. It is making taste repeatable.
slides.job: Frame the commercial problem as repeatable taste, not slide generation volume.

slides.title: Data changes the deck only when it becomes design memory.
slides.job: Contrast raw source collection with usable design memory that changes layout decisions.

slides.title: The skill chooses what to show, what to remove, and what to gate.
slides.job: Reveal the skill as a staged deck director that routes detail and preserves governance.

slides.title: Run 2.0 earns the climax only when measured results support it.
slides.job: Reserve the largest visual field for the measured experiment result and make the evaluation delta unmistakable once results exist.

slides.title: Public-ready is a gate, not a vibe.
slides.job: Close on release governance, render verification, and human approval.
